nodes.label: AI as efficient complete solution provider
nodes.grounding_span: ideally that takes less time when it works

nodes.label: AI as systematic troubleshooter
nodes.grounding_span: it can be valuable at times, but it's not common, and usually the troubleshooting process is mostly a necessary detour

nodes.label: AI as supplement to creative process
nodes.grounding_span: I will ask AI for small things like a list of unique character names

nodes.label: AI as research starting point
nodes.grounding_span: it is just becomes another tool for me to cross-examine rather than the definitive research solution

nodes.label: Openness about AI use with colleagues
nodes.label_negative: Discreet AI use due to cultural stigma
nodes.grounding_span: me and some other people are using AI discreetly without fully embracing it on a team-wide level

nodes.label: AI as lore-aware assistant for game development
nodes.label_negative: AI lacking knowledge of niche game lore
nodes.grounding_span: I can imagine really cool scenarios where we may even build our own AI bot that knows all of our game knowledge

nodes.label: Efficiency and time savings
nodes.grounding_span: ideally that takes less time when it works

nodes.label: Creative autonomy
nodes.grounding_span: it makes me feel like I have more ownership over it

nodes.label: Epistemic accuracy and understanding
nodes.grounding_span: I have to take it with a large grain of salt

nodes.label: Team cohesion and shared standards
nodes.grounding_span: I hope there are new ways to allow AI to become a part of the 'social-norm' and accepted by people in the creative industry

nodes.label: Satisfaction with AI for debugging
nodes.grounding_span: it can be valuable at times, but it's not common, and usually the troubleshooting process is mostly a necessary detour

nodes.label: Resistance to AI in creative work
nodes.grounding_span: I wouldn't usually ask AI to give me ideas on what a certain trailer or cutscene should provide

nodes.label: Frustration with AI for niche problems
nodes.grounding_span: the AI will definitely say 'There is no X in game' and yet when I look at human provided resources...it will prove otherwise

nodes.label: Hope for AI as definitive research tool
nodes.grounding_span: Ideally, I would like AI to hopefully one-day relatively soon to become the definitive research solution

nodes.label: Frustration with AI's lack of cultural nuance
nodes.grounding_span: I am worried on the cultural push-back on it

nodes.label: Iterative problem-solving — start simple, escalate if needed
nodes.grounding_span: I tend to at first usually try to give it as much context as possible...But usually it does not get the complete solution on its own, so I have to then go step-by-step

nodes.label: Domain-based boundary setting — separates creative from non-creative tasks
nodes.grounding_span: Some higher-level story ideas I prefer to do myself...I will ask AI for small things like a list of unique character names
